overview.tagline: A quiet, family-friendly borough in northern Bergen County
overview.description: Park Ridge is a small, suburban borough in the northwestern corner of Bergen County known for its excellent schools, safe neighborhoods, and strong sense of community. With tree-lined streets, well-maintained parks, and a quaint downtown, Park Ridge offers a peaceful residential setting while remaining within commuting distance of New York City. The borough borders Rockland County, New York, and is near the Ramapo Mountains.
highlights: Highly rated public schools in a small-town setting
Proximity to hiking and outdoor recreation in the Ramapo Mountains
Active volunteer fire department and community organizations
Easy access to the Garden State Parkway and major highways

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Park Ridge features predominantly single-family homes on modest to mid-sized lots.
realEstate.recentTrends: Park Ridge has experienced steady appreciation in home values driven by its excellent school district and family-friendly reputation. The market favors single-family homes, and inventory tends to be limited, leading to competitive bidding in desirable neighborhoods.
neighborhoodNotes: Downtown area — Close to shops and restaurants along Park Avenue
Kinderkamack Road corridor — Mixed residential and commercial properties
Northern section — Larger lots near the Woodcliff Lake border
Southern section — Established neighborhoods close to Pascack Brook County Park

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Park Ridge Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic #1-#7) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same recycling day
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through November
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water/Suez)
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia on a quarterly basis
Sewer charges included on property tax bills
Emergency water service available 24/7 through Veolia

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board of Adjustment
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificates of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough operates municipal parking lots and commuter parking near the train station.
details: Commuter parking permits available for NJ Transit station lot
Municipal parking available in downtown area
Overnight parking restrictions on borough streets
Snow emergency parking regulations enforced
Parking permits available through Borough Clerk

additionalServices.title: Park Ridge Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library offering books, digital resources, programs, and community spaces.
details: Located at 20 Ridge Road
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and study rooms
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and seasonal programs
Summer camp and playground programs
Adult fitness and senior programs
Community events throughout the year

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Park Ridge enforces noise regulations to maintain its quiet residential character.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers restricted to certain hours during fall season
Loud music and gatherings subject to noise limits after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in rear yards and 4 feet in front yards
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed from property
Trees and vegetation must not obstruct sidewalks or sight lines

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Park Ridge requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Barking dogs are subject to noise complaints and violations
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Snow emergency parking bans enforced — vehicles must be off-street
Commuter parking lots require valid permits
No commercial vehicle parking on residential streets
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Short-term rentals (under 30 days) are not permitted in residential zones
Rental property inspections conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Yard Sales
other.summary: Garage and yard sales are permitted with restrictions.
keyRules: Permits required for yard/garage sales
Limited to a maximum number of sales per year
Signs must be removed within 24 hours after the sale
Sales may only be held during designated hours

publicSchools.name: West Ridge Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school serving the younger students of the district

publicSchools.name: East Brook Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Upper elementary and middle school combining grades 3 through 8

publicSchools.name: Park Ridge High School
publicSchools.notes: Small high school known for strong academics and competitive athletics

privateSchools.name: Our Lady of Mercy Academy
privateSchools.notes: Catholic parish school serving the Park Ridge community

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 15 minutes

whyMoveHere: Excellent, small-town public school district with low student-to-teacher ratios
Safe, quiet residential neighborhoods ideal for families
Close to hiking, parks, and outdoor recreation in the Ramapo Mountains
Strong community spirit with active civic organizations
Easy highway access via Garden State Parkway

commuteToNYC.duration: 50-65 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH or ferry to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.notes: The Park Ridge train station is on the Pascack Valley Line, offering service to Hoboken Terminal. Bus 165 provides service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan. The commute is approximately 60-80 minutes by bus depending on traffic.

costOfLiving.summary: Park Ridge offers a moderate cost of living by Bergen County standards, with lower home prices than many southern Bergen communities but higher property taxes relative to home values.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 25% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Excellent schools with small class sizes
Proximity to Ramapo Mountains for outdoor recreation
Reasonable home prices compared to other Bergen County towns
NJ Transit train service to Hoboken
cons: Longer commute to NYC than southern Bergen County towns
Higher property taxes relative to home values
Small downtown with limited retail options
Train service runs on a more limited schedule than main line

restaurants.name: Park Ridge Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with a wide-ranging American menu and friendly atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Aldo & Gianni Ristorante
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant offering traditional pasta dishes, seafood, and house-made specialties in an upscale setting.

restaurants.name: Kinchley's Tavern
restaurants.description: Local institution known for thin-crust pizza and a casual, laid-back tavern atmosphere. A Bergen County favorite.

restaurants.name: Thai Chef
restaurants.description: Popular Thai restaurant serving authentic dishes including curries, pad Thai, and noodle soups.

parks.name: Pascack Brook County Park
parks.description: Bergen County park featuring walking trails along Pascack Brook, sports fields, playgrounds, and picnic areas.

parks.name: Veterans Park
parks.description: Borough memorial park with walking paths, benches, and a peaceful setting honoring local veterans.

parks.name: Heritage Park
parks.description: Community park featuring playground equipment, sports courts, and open green space for family activities.

events.name: Park Ridge Fall Festival
events.description: Annual community festival with food vendors, live music, games, crafts, and family entertainment.
events.when: October, typically a Saturday in early October

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade honoring veterans and active military, featuring local organizations, bands, and community groups.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting ceremony, caroling, refreshments, and visits from Santa.

attractions.name: Pascack Brook County Park
attractions.description: Scenic county park with trails, sports facilities, and natural areas along Pascack Brook, popular for walking and nature watching.

attractions.name: Park Ridge Historical Society
attractions.description: Local historical society preserving the borough's heritage through exhibits, programs, and archival collections.

shoppingAreas: Park Avenue — Downtown shops and services
Kinderkamack Road — Commercial corridor with restaurants and retail
Woodcliff Lake shopping — Adjacent town with additional retail
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major regional shopping center nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 53 Park Avenue, Park Ridge, NJ 07656

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
